Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports engineering of flavonoid apioside production by increasing precursor supply for liquiritigenin/isoliquiritigenin-derived apiosides; indirect value for medicinal/nutritional flavonoid enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone intermediate in flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Malonyl-CoA as extender substrate; CoA-linked starter substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor flux toward (iso)liquiritigenin and downstream (iso)liquiritin apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis as bait gene; pathway reconstruction context in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Higher expression in roots of Glycyrrhiza uralensis where (iso)liquiritin apiosides accumulate
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Assigned as known key flavonoid biosynthetic enzyme and used as bait gene in co-expression analysis; included in engineered module 1
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts upstream of liquiritigenin/isoliquiritigenin shown in proposed pathway figure
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene used as co-expression bait
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Glycyrrhiza uralensis root/cortex/leaf transcriptomes; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue comparison among roots, cortexes, and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS of liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside in plant tissues; engineered production analysis
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Used to identify co-expressed UGT candidates rather than newly functionally characterized here
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomes of G. uralensis tissues; SRA BioProject PRJNA945816
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core flavonoid/chalcone branch leading to liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Roots, cortexes, leaves; tobacco leaves in reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Can be used to increase precursor supply for flavonoid apiosides in metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces chalcone intermediates in the legume-specific pathway contributing to isoliquiritigenin/liquiritigenin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of (iso)liquiritigenin precursors for downstream apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis as bait gene; pathway reconstruction context in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Highly expressed in roots where target apiosides accumulate
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known pathway enzyme used as bait gene; part of proposed pathway and engineering module
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Shown upstream of isoliquiritigenin/liquiritigenin in proposed biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene used as co-expression bait
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: G. uralensis tissue transcriptomes; transiently transformed tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS of tissue metabolites; engineered production analysis
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Not newly cloned/functionally dissected here
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced chalcone intermediate contributing to isoliquiritigenin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point not established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Not central to flavonoid apioside engineering in this study; mainly used to exclude alternative UGT candidates

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No direct effect on flavonoid apiosides in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior literature annotation; excluded during candidate filtering
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Mentioned in candidate UGT annotation stage
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Previously reported triterpenoid glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Annotated as non-target UGT distinct from flavonoid apiosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Annotated from transcriptome databases and prior report

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosyltransferase acting in triterpenoid specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No demonstrated role in flavonoid apioside production here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior literature annotation; excluded from focal apiosyltransferase discovery
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Mentioned during annotation of candidate UGTs
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Previously reported triterpenoid glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Annotated as unrelated UGT in candidate set
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Annotated from transcriptomic candidates and prior report
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: No new functional data in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High value for metabolic engineering and production of bioactive flavonoid apiosides such as liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside; useful for synthetic biology and medicinal quality improvement
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to the 2''-hydroxyl of flavonoid glycosides to form 2''-O-apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Uses UDP-apiose as sugar donor; no divalent metal required and some divalent cations suppress activity; UDP-apiose supplied in assays via UAXS, UDP-GlcA, and NAD+
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonoid apiosides including liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR cloning, recombinant protein assay, LC/MS product identification, comparison with standards, kinetic analysis, substrate screening, crystal structure, mutagenesis, MD/QM simulations, transient pathway reconstruction in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Highly expressed in G. uralensis roots where flavonoid apiosides are enriched; active in heterologous tobacco leaves during transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant enzyme almost completely converted liquiritin and isoliquiritin into their 2''-O-apiosides; products confirmed by LC/MS, standards, and NMR for multiple products
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Direct conversion of (iso)liquiritin to (iso)liquiritin apiosides established by in vitro assays and de novo pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank OQ201607; UGT nomenclature UGT79B74; PDB 8HZZ
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression analysis of tissue transcriptomes using GuCHS and GuCHR as bait genes, annotation filtering, phylogenetic analysis, RT-PCR cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: G. uralensis root/cortex/leaf transcriptomes (n=2 each tissue); Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Root-enriched expression; pH optimum 8.0 and temperature optimum 37°C for assays
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Site: R368, L369, G370, S371, D372, H373, I136 important for donor selectivity; S371/H373Q and L369/H373Q enable UDP-Xyl acceptance; I136T/G370/H373Q enables UDP-Glc acceptance
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: Site-directed amino acid substitutions / motif engineering
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS, HPLC/UV, HR-ESI-MS, 1D/2D NMR for selected products
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Displays strict donor selectivity for UDP-apiose among tested donors; unique 45-aa PSPG box with RLGSDH motif underlies selectivity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomes PRJNA945816; candidate gene accessions OR372660-OR372775 for related ApiGTs; crystal structure PDB 8HZZ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Broad substrate promiscuity toward 37 phenolic glycosides; no acceptance of free flavonoids or triterpenoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glycosyl modification branch of flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some mutations altering donor specificity may change pocket rigidity/selectivity; no agronomic tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 2''-O-apiosylated flavonoid glycosides such as liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonoid glycosides such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin plus UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; GT-B fold; 45-aa PSPG box with RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside reached 5.46 and 4.73 mg/g DW in engineered tobacco; 14 substrates showed >80% conversion; Km for isoliquiritin 2.59±0.23 µM; kcat 0.11 s−1
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Liquiritin apioside, isoliquiritin apioside, and related flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Content/accumulation and de novo biosynthetic production
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: G. uralensis roots/cortexes/leaves; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Essential donor-supply enzyme for engineering apiosylated nutraceuticals and flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucuronic acid into UDP-apiose and UDP-xylose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to supply UDP-apiose donor for apiosyltransferase-mediated accumulation of flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coupled enzyme assays supplying UDP-apiose; preparation/purification of UDP-apiose; tobacco co-expression with GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Used in vitro and in transiently engineered tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known enzyme from Arabidopsis thaliana introduced to generate UDP-apiose in catalytic systems; product donor supported GuApiGT reactions
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Directly generates the activated sugar donor used by GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known donor-supply gene used as pathway component
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Enzyme-coupled assays; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC purification/UV 262 nm for UDP-apiose; LC/MS of downstream apioside products
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis thaliana UAXS was used in most assays because UDP-apiose is commercially unavailable
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Simultaneous production of UDP-xylose could potentially compete, although GuApiGT did not use UDP-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: In vitro reaction mixtures; tobacco leaves

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid glycosylation / engineered apiosylation specificity study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful scaffold for protein engineering of glycosyltransferases to create apiosylated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Native enzyme catalyzes glycosylation of flavonoid aglycones at the 3-O position; engineered mutants gained ability to transfer apiose to kaempferol
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Uses UDP-sugars; WT accepts at least five sugar donors except UDP-apiose; mutant assays used UDP-apiose supply system with UAXS, UDP-GlcA, NAD+
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered mutants enable formation of kaempferol 3-O-apioside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure of Sb3GT1/UDP, site-directed mutagenesis, enzyme assay, LC/MS product detection, crystal structure of mutant/UDP-Glc complex
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: In vitro enzyme engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: WT known as efficient flavonoid 3-O-glycosyltransferase; mutants produced kaempferol 3-O-apioside detected by characteristic aglycone ion
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Direct glycosylation/apiosylation of kaempferol in enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Engineered mutants 375S/Q377H, F372R/Q376H, and F372R/375S/Q377H for gained apiosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected known glycosyltransferase for rational engineering based on structural comparison
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mutagenesis of FFGDQ motif corresponding to GuApiGT RLGSDH motif
Pathway_Genes.Key_Variant_Type: Site-directed amino acid substitutions and insertion
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Used as proof-of-principle to demonstrate sufficiency of enlarged motif for apiosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may alter native donor/acceptor preferences

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential enzyme for engineering apiosylated flavonoids in legume crops and medicinal plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Apiosyltransferase catalyzing transfer of apiose from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid apioside formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning and recombinant enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Identified from species reported to contain flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Identified as apiosyltransferase by enzyme catalysis reactions
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Functionally grouped with GuApiGT and related legume ApiGTs
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank OQ230795 or within OQ230794-OQ230797 range; exact mapping not fully specified
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology search using GuApiGT and unique 45-aa PSPG box; RT-PCR cloning

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Homology search from online transcriptome databases; related candidate accessions OR372660-OR372775

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis in engineered tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful but suboptimal glycosyltransferase for reconstructing flavonoid apioside biosynthesis; demonstrates importance of regioselective glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates flavonoid aglycones/intermediates to produce glucosides, but with poor regioselectivity in tobacco system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled formation of glucosylated precursor but generated major byproduct 7a, reducing clean production of target apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana with LC/MS/HPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Previously reported glycosyltransferase used in module 3; in vivo reconstruction produced glucosylated intermediates and byproduct pattern
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts before GuApiGT by forming glycosylated acceptors required for apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously reported candidate used in pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with module 1 and 2 genes in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS and HPLC chromatograms of tobacco extracts
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Replaced by GuGT53 because of poor regioselectivity and interference from endogenous tobacco glycosyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonoid O-glycosylation branch upstream of apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Target yield improved when replaced by GuGT53

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid glycoside biosynthesis leading to flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High utility for constructing efficient pathway to bioactive liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside in plants or microbial/plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regio-selectively transfers glucose to the 4'/4-O position of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin to produce liquiritin and isoliquiritin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases supply of correct glucoside acceptors for GuApiGT and improves isoliquiritin apioside production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Discovery by co-expression pattern, cloning, recombinant enzyme assay, transient pathway reconstruction with improved yields
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Transient expression in tobacco leaves during de novo pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Recombinant GuGT53 efficiently and regio-selectively converted liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin into liquiritin and isoliquiritin
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts directly upstream of GuApiGT because products liquiritin/isoliquiritin are apiosylated by GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression-guided discovery from G. uralensis transcriptome and functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: G. uralensis transcriptomes; transiently engineered Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT; tobacco transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS, HPLC in tobacco extracts and enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Replacement of GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved target apioside yield by correcting regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Reduced byproduct formation relative to GuGT14
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavanone/chalcone O-glucosylation branch immediately upstream of apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Improved isoliquiritin apioside yield in tobacco relative to GuGT14-based module
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant not established; immediate precursor-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Liquiritin, isoliquiritin, and downstream liquiritin apioside / isoliquiritin apioside
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Correct precursor formation and final product accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: General upstream engineering target to enhance flavonoid and specialized metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies initial phenylpropanoid precursor flux for engineered production of flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstruction in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used as standard known pathway enzyme in module 1
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Most upstream step in engineered pathway from phenylalanine to flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known Arabidopsis pathway gene used in synthetic module
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: LC/MS of final apioside products in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Engineering module component rather than discovery target
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Final product accumulation via upstream flux

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway reconstruction in tobacco

Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known Arabidopsis pathway enzyme used in module 1
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Early phenylpropanoid step upstream of 4CL and CHS

Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Engineering component only

Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Liquiritin apioside not detected when AtCHI was used

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports synthetic biology production of apiosylated flavonoids by enhancing UDP-glucose precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate and glucose-6-phosphate in the phosphoglucomutase step to support UDP-glucose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Mg2+ typically required for phosphoglucomutase activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to UDP-glucose and thus UDP-apiose donor supply in engineered tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use as module 2 component in transient tobacco reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used as known enzyme in donor module producing UDP-Glc and UDP-Api
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Upstream of GalU and UAXS in donor-supply pathway
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene selected for synthetic module
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with GalU, CalS8, and UAXS
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Included solely as donor-supply support in module 2

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports donor-sugar biosynthesis for engineered apiosylated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate and UTP to UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides UDP-glucose precursor for downstream UDP-apiose biosynthesis and glucosylation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use in tobacco donor module
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used as known pathway enzyme in donor module
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Direct producer of UDP-Glc upstream of CalS8 and UAXS
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known gene selected for synthetic module
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Module component only

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Supports donor-sugar pathway engineering for apiosylated flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Enzyme used upstream of UAXS in the engineered pathway to produce UDP-GlcA from UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to UDP-glucuronic acid supply for UDP-apiose production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use in tobacco module 2
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used as established donor-module enzyme between UDP-Glc and UAXS-supported UDP-Api formation
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Upstream of UAXS in donor module
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known gene selected for donor-supply engineering
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with Pgm, GalU, and UAXS
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Likely included to increase UDP-GlcA supply, but exact catalytic details were not elaborated in main text

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavone branch of flavonoid apioside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Expands engineered production portfolio to additional flavone apiosides in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones to flavones by desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables de novo biosynthesis of additional flavone apiosides in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Alternative module-1 gene combinations in transient tobacco production
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Used to generate flavone skeletons for production of eight additional apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts upstream of glycosylation and apiosylation in flavone branch
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known pathway gene selected for modular engineering
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Included to broaden chemical diversity of apiosides produced
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Additional flavonoid apiosides produced at 0.19–6.25 mg/g DW with different module combinations

Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step inflavonoid apiosides biosynthesis ofLeguminosae plants
